name: Virto Commerce B2B E-Commerce API
what_it_does: Open-source B2B and B2C e-commerce platform with REST API — enabling enterprise teams to build complex commercial ecosystems with multi-catalog, multi-store, multi-currency, complex pricing and promotions, quote requests, negotiated pricing, and headless delivery via REST API, built on ASP.NET Core with a modular microservices architecture.
use_cases: Managing Virto Commerce product catalogs and pricing via API for enterprise catalog automation
Processing B2B quote requests and custom pricing via Virto Commerce API for sales workflow
Managing Virto Commerce multi-store and multi-tenant setups via API for enterprise operations
Integrating Virto Commerce with ERP and PIM systems via API for data synchronization
Building headless Virto Commerce storefronts via REST API for custom B2B buyer portals
not_for: Simple B2C stores (Virto Commerce complexity is overkill for simple stores)
Teams without .NET/C# expertise (Virto Commerce is ASP.NET Core)
Small businesses needing quick setup (enterprise configuration is complex)
best_when: An enterprise B2B company needs a fully customizable e-commerce platform with complex pricing, quote management, multi-catalog, and ERP integration — where a .NET team can leverage the modular microservices architecture for deep customization.
avoid_when: You're building a simple B2C store, don't have .NET expertise, or need quick managed SaaS setup.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with IdentityServer. Bearer token auth. Scoped access per module (catalog, orders, customers, pricing). Webhook for commerce events (order created, status changed). JavaScript SDK and .NET SDK. Multi-tenant with store-level access control. Role-based permissions (admin, manager, buyer). API versioning via URL path.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source (Virto Commerce Open Source License) — free community edition
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise license + support from $25,000/year; SaaS from $1,000/month
pricing.notes: American company (Los Angeles). Founded 2011 by Alexander Siniouguine. Open source (Virto Commerce OSL). GitHub: VirtoCommerce/vc-platform (1,200+ stars). ASP.NET Core + .NET 8. Microservices architecture (optional). Modularity: 100+ modules. B2B-specialized: complex pricing, quote requests, negotiated pricing, approval workflows, buyer portals. Used by: manufacturers, distributors, industrial companies. Multi-tenant SaaS for commerce operators. Deployed on Azure, AWS, on-premises. Gartner recognized. US, Europe, and APAC customers.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — no platform limits; SaaS rate limits by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with OAuth2 + IdentityServer. Webhook for commerce events. JS and .NET SDKs. Complex B2B pricing requires understanding Virto's price list model. Annual contract for enterprise. .NET expertise required. Open source but complex to set up.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Product reads idempotent by ID or code. Order reads idempotent by ID. Price reads idempotent. Entity creation not idempotent — check by code/SKU. OAuth2 tokens refresh automatically.
known_agent_gotchas: Complex B2B pricing model (price lists, tier pricing, negotiated pricing) requires deep understanding
Microservices deployment optional but complex — modular monolith also supported
Quote request workflow requires B2B-specific API patterns
Annual enterprise contract — expensive for small deployments

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. PCI-DSS and GDPR compliant. OAuth2 with IdentityServer (industry standard). Scoped access per module. Role-based permissions with fine-grained controls. Open source — auditable ASP.NET Core code. User-controlled data residency (self-hosted). Azure, AWS, or on-premises deployment options. Multi-tenant isolation at application level.
